Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me first calculate the duration for each case by looking at the first Submit Claim and last Close Claim timestamps Case 2001 Start 202404010900 End 202404011030 Duration 1 hour 30 minutes very fast Case 2002 Start 202404010905 End 202402021100 Duration 1 day 1 hour 55 minutes about 25 hours 55 minutes Case 2003 Start 202404010910 End 202404030930 Duration 2 days 0 hours 20 minutes about 48 hours 20 minutes Case 2004 Start 202404010920 End 202404011045 Duration 1 hour 25 minutes very fast Case 2005 Start 202404010925 End 202404041430 Duration 3 days 5 hours 5 minutes about 77 hours So significantly longer cases 2003 High Region A 2005 High Region B and 2002 Medium Region B Fast cases 2001 Low Region A 2004 Low Region B

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Let me calculate more precise durations 2001 15 hours 2004 125 hours 2002 From 0905 Apr 1 to 1100 Apr 2 Thats 25 hours 55 minutes 2003 From 0910 Apr 1 to 0930 Apr 3 Thats 48 hours 20 minutes 2005 From 0925 Apr 1 to 1430 Apr 4 Thats 3 days 5 hours 5 minutes 77 hours 5 minutes Now lets look at attributes Complexity correlation Low 2001 15h 2004 125h Fast Medium 2002 26h Slow High 2003 48h 2005 77h Very slow multiple document requests Region correlation Region A 2001 Low fast 2003 High slow Region B 2002 Medium slow 2004 Low fast 2005 High very slow Region alone doesnt seem to be the main differentiator

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets look at resources Case 2001 Low A 15h CSRJane AdjusterMike ManagerAnn FinanceAlan Case 2002 Medium B 26h CSRPaul AdjusterLisa ManagerAnn FinanceCarl Document request by AdjusterLisa Case 2003 High A 48h CSRJane AdjusterMike ManagerBill FinanceAlan Document requests by AdjusterMike twice Note ManagerBill instead of ManagerAnn Case 2004 Low B 125h CSRMary AdjusterLisa ManagerAnn FinanceCarl Case 2005 High B 77h CSRPaul AdjusterLisa ManagerBill FinanceCarl Document requests by AdjusterLisa three times Note ManagerBill instead of ManagerAnn Interesting observations 1 When ManagerAnn approves cases seem faster 2001 ManagerAnn 15h 2002 ManagerAnn 26h but medium 2004 ManagerAnn 125h 2003 ManagerBill 48h high 2005 ManagerBill 77h high So ManagerBill only handles High complexity cases here Cant conclude hes slower its confounded by complexity 2 AdjusterMike vs AdjusterLisa AdjusterMike 2001 Low fast no doc 2003 High slow 2 doc AdjusterLisa 2002 Medium slow 1 doc 2004 Low fast no doc 2005 High very slow 3 doc Both handle based on complexity but AdjusterLisa took 3 requests for case 2005

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Root cause analysis Primary factor Complexity Low No additional document requests completed same day 15h Medium One document request completed in 26h spans overnight High Multiple document requests completed in 23 days Secondary factor Multipleiterative document requests Case 2002 Medium 1 doc request 1day delay Case 2003 High 2 doc requests 2day delay Case 2005 High 3 doc requests 3 day delay Tertiary factor ResourceRegion interactions Region A High 2003 took 48h with AdjusterMike and 2 requests Region B High 2005 took 77h with AdjusterLisa and 3 requests Is AdjusterLisa less efficient or just variation The presence of Request Additional Documents is the bottleneck High complexity claims trigger this activity multiple times The waiting time between document requests and subsequent activities spans hours or overnight

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me look at waiting times more carefully Case 2002 Evaluate 0945 Apr 1 Request Docs 1400 Apr 1 4h15m after evaluate Request at 1400 Apr 1 Approve at 1000 Apr 2 Gap 20 hours Case 2003 Evaluate 0940 Apr 1 Request 1 1100 Apr 1 1h20m later Request 2 1700 Apr 1 6h after first Approve 1600 Apr 2 23h after last request Payment Apr 3 0900 17h after approval Case 2005 Evaluate 0950 Apr 1 Request 1 1130 Apr 1 1h40m later Request 2 1700 Apr 2 295h Request 3 1500 Apr 3 22h after second Approve 1000 Apr 4 19h after third

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The Request Additional Documents activities are actions BY the insurer Adjuster In Case 2003 AdjusterMike requested docs at 1100 then AGAIN at 1700 This implies either a The first request didnt get a response so remindersecond request b Or docs came back but insufficient requiring another request For Case 2005 AdjusterLisa requested docs three times This clearly indicates a struggle to get complete documentation So root causes 1 Complexity is the strongest predictor High complexity leads to document requests extending lead time 2 Iterativeinefficient document collection process Multiple requests create waiting time customer response time and rework loops High complexity cases have more iterations 3 Potential resource region variation Case 2005 Region B High took significantly longer than Case 2003 Region A High with 3 doc requests vs 2 This could suggest AdjusterLisa Region B may not be requesting the right documents upfront or Region B customers less responsive However sample size is small

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Also note payment delays Case 2003 Approved Apr 2 1600 Paid Apr 3 0900 overnight delay FinanceAlan Case 2005 Approved Apr 4 1000 Paid Apr 4 1400 same day FinanceCarl Looking at ManagerAnn vs ManagerBill ManagerAnn handles 20012002 2004 all LowMedium ManagerBill handles 20032005 both High This suggests ManagerBill might be assigned to high complexity cases approval happens quickly after last doc request

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Let me doublecheck if theres any other pattern Is there a weekend involved April 1 2024 was a Monday April 2 Tuesday April 3 Wednesday April 4 Thursday So no weekend delay The delays are all businessday related Also look at the timestamps for requests in Case 2005 Request 1 Apr 1 1130 Request 2 Apr 2 1700 next day evening almost 30 hours later Request 3 Apr 3 1500 next day afternoon 22 hours later This pattern suggests that AdjusterLisa reviews the case once per day approximately If the customer submits docs Lisa reviews them the next working day finds them insufficient and sends another request For Case 2003 AdjusterMike Request 1 Apr 1 1100 Request 2 Apr 1 1700 same day So the difference between Mike and Lisa Mike managed to do two requests in one day while Lisa spaced them across three separate days This supports the AdjusterLisa review cadence efficiency hypothesis
